title: Initialization, Indeterminate Values, and Undefined Behavior · Invariant Recall
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the defined-state initialization audit contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for defined-state initialization audit after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Initialize every read-reachable scalar, member, padding-sensitive operation, and error-path state before use.
Avoid evaluating indeterminate values and understand when unsigned-char inspection is and is not permitted.
Explain how undefined behavior removes semantic constraints and invalidates debug-build intuition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
